design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based combat with exploration, character recruitment, and town-building. Players advance through story-driven dungeons while building a village with special NPCs and shamans who grant party member transformations.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mystery-driven narrative (protagonist's family vanished, townspeople forgot him) balanced against growing party power through shaman fusion system and village development.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Build a legendary party of diverse fantasy races (dog-people, cat-people, winged humans, dragon clan member) while constructing and populating your own settlement, discovering what happened to your family.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong narrative continuity as direct sequel (500 years after original); distinctive town-building mechanic that rewards exploration for NPC recruitment; shaman fusion system creates strategic party customization through elemental alignment choices; Personal Actions tied to specific characters gate progression through puzzle/exploration design.

difficulty.checkpointing: Battery back-up save system at dragon statues throughout gameplay
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Formation system allows player to adjust party positioning for tactical advantages (speed, defense, attack power); character selection and party composition (limited to 4 of 9 recruitable characters) affects combat outcomes; shaman fusion combinations provide strategic depth

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling granting new spells and stat increases; shaman fusion system where up to 2 elemental shamans (6 total available) can bond with party members for stat boosts and new abilities; equipment via combat rewards and town interaction
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character recruitment at set story points with unique Personal Actions (puzzle-solving, object destruction, area access); shaman recruitment through exploration; village expansion via currency donation to carpenters (three distinct building styles); new spells unlock via leveling
replayability_hooks: Nine recruitable characters but only 4-party limit creates different party composition playthroughs
Six shamans with elemental alignment to fuse selectively offers varied stat/ability builds
Three carpenter building styles suggest aesthetic player agency in village customization
Monster Meter mechanic implies variable encounter density across playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Direct sequel continuity (500 years post-original game) establishes deep lore
Mystery narrative frame (protagonist family vanishment, memory erasure) drives character motivation
Character-specific Personal Actions reinforce individual identity and role in world
Shaman elemental system reflects broader magical world structure
Town-building mechanic serves as passive world-building through NPC recruitment dialogue and profession variety

faction_indicators: Dragon Clan (Ryu's lineage, draconic transformation abilities)
Winged humans (Nina - princess with black wings as exile marker)
Six elemental shamans with distinct alignments
danger_signals: All townspeople suffered collective memory loss regarding protagonist
Nina exiled for black wings (considered 'bad omen' in her homeland)
Story centers on clearing false accusation against protagonist's friend
environmental_storytelling: Town construction through player agency shows settlement growth reflecting player choices
Dragon statues as save points establish religious/cultural significance
Dangerous areas forced by story progression mark threat locations
Character recruitment across diverse locations suggests fragmented post-disaster world
Coliseum setting for Katt suggests organized society with combat traditions
